ANY LEGAL PREJUDICE TO MR. MCNERNEY AND VERITAS BY THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION IS ELIMINATED IF THE TRIAL IS CONTINUED TO MAY OR JUNE 2019 AS MINT COLLECTION EXPECTS WILL HAPPEN, BUT HAS NOT YET OCCURRED NOR IS THERE A PENDING MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL. ABSENT A TRIAL CONTINUANCE, THE PREJUDICE TO MR. MCNERNEY AND VERITAS FROM HAVING TO PREPARE FOR A TRIAL TWO MONTHS AWAY AND BEING DEPRIVED OF THE OPPORTUNITY TO FILE A SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION REQUIRES DENIAL OF THE MOTION. IF THE TRIAL IS CONTINUED, GRANTING THE MOTION COMPORTS WITH CALIFORNIA'S LIBERAL POLICY OF ALLOWING AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS AT ANY STAGE IN A CASE. MINT COLLECTION'S ALTER EGO ALLEGATIONS, EVEN IF NOT LEGALLY SUFFICIENT, DO NOT SHOW THAT MINT COLLECTION WILL BE UNABLE TO VIABLY ALLEGE ALTER EGO LIABILITY AGAINST MR. MCNERNEY AND VERITAS. COUNSEL FOR MINT COLLECTION IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD E. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MARTIN MCNERNEY DEVELOPMENT, INC., FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, 418 JESSIE HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, AND 418 JESSIE PROPERTIES, LLC'S SPECIAL DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - HEARING HELD, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED: MBC-RELATED DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE ENTIRETY OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT AND TO THE FIRST, EIGHTH AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION THEREIN IS: A) SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE ENTIRETY OF THE SAC; B) SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF RESIDENTIAL BUILDING STANDARDS AND THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR UNLAWFUL LLC DISTRIBUTIONS; AND C) OVERRULED AS TO THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. BECAUSE THERE ARE FOUR DIFFERENT MBC-RELATED PARTIES, EACH WITH WHAT APPEARS TO BE A DIFFERENT ROLE REGARDING THE REAL PROPERTY THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF THIS CASE, AS TO EACH CAUSE OF ACTION PLAINTIFF MINT COLLECTION-410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM OWNERS' ASSOCIATION SHOULD ALLEGE WHY IT CONTENDS EACH OF THESE DEFENDANTS IS LIABLE INSTEAD OF LUMPING THEM ALL TOGETHER IN A COLLECTIVELY DEFINED TERM. NOTWITHSTANDING THAT IT WAS GIVEN ANOTHER OPPORTUNITY TO ALLEGE VIABLE CLAMS FOR VIOLATION OF THE RESIDENTIAL BUILDING STANDARDS AND UNLAWFUL LLC DISTRIBUTIONS, MINT COLLECTION HAS NOT DONE AND IT NOW APPEARS THAT IT IS UNABLE TO DO SO. THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES NOT DISCLOSE ON ITS FACE THAT ALL OR ANY PORTION OF THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM IS NECESSARILY TIME-BARRED. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE ORDER FOR FULL DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM. =(302/HEK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MARTIN MCNERNEY DEVELOPMENT, INC., FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, 418 JESSIE HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, AND 418 JESSIE PROPERTIES, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - HEARING HELD. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED: MBC-RELATED DEFENDANTS MOTION TO STRIKE THE ENTIRETY AND SPECIFIC PORTIONS OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. WHILE THE BETTER COURSE WOULD HAVE BEEN EITHER NOT TO RE-PLEAD THE STRICT LIABILITY AND BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY CLAIMS OR TO EXPLICITLY STATE THAT THEY WERE RE-PLEAD FOR APPELLATE PRESERVATION PURPOSES ONLY, BECAUSE PLAINTIFF MINT COLLECTION-410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM OWNERS' ASSOCIATION DOES NOT MAINTAIN THAT THOSE CLAIMS REMAIN VIABLE IN THIS COURT, THERE IS NO REASON TO STRIKE THEM. MINT COLLECTION'S PLEADING OF ALTER EGO ALLEGATIONS IS NOT IMPERMISSIBLE IN THE WAY IT DID SO. AT THIS POINT IN THE CASE THE ALLEGATIONS AND PRAYER SEEKING ATTORNEY'S FEES, EXPERT FEES AND COSTS IS LARGELY IMMATERIAL AND THE COURT CANNOT SAY WITH CERTITUDE THAT MINT COLLECTION WILL NOT BE ENTITLED TO ATTORNEY'S FEES, EXPERT FEES AND COSTS BASED ON POST-JUDGMENT MOTIONS. COUNSEL FOR MINT COLLECTION IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD E. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RAINBOW WATERPROOFING & RESTORATION CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO JUNE 25, 2019. DEFENDANT AND CROSS-DEFENDANT RAINBOW WATERPROOFING & RESTORATION CO. FILED ITS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON FEBRUARY 15, 2019. ON APRIL 25, IT FILED A MOTION FOR A GOOD FAITH DETERMINATION AS TO ITS SETTLEMENT WITH PLAINTIFF, WHICH IS CURRENTLY SET FOR HEARING ON JUNE 5, 2019. ACCORDING TO COUNSEL TO THE DEVELOPER DEFENDANTS AND PATRICK MCNERNEY, RAINBOW REFUSED THEIR REQUESTS TO WITHDRAW ITS SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION OR TO CONTINUE THE HEARING DATE ON THAT MOTION PENDING THE COURT'S DECISION ON THE GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT MOTION SO AS TO ALLOW THEM TO AVOID PREPARING AND FILING AN OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION THAT MIGHT BE UNNECESSARY OR MOOTED IN PART. ON APRIL 29, HOWEVER, RAINBOW THEN FILED AN EX PARTE APPLICATION SEEKING TO CONTINUE THE HEARING ON THE SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION. HOWEVER, ITS COUNSEL FAILED TO APPEAR ON THE COURT'S EX PARTE CALENDAR ON APRIL 30, THE DAY BEFORE THE SCHEDULED HEARING ON THE MOTION. THE HEARING IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION BECAUSE THE DISPOSITION OF THE SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION NECESSARILY WILL DEPEND IN PART ON THE COURT'S RULING ON THE GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT MOTION. HOWEVER, THE COURT ADMONISHES RAINBOW THAT ITS CONDUCT HAS CAUSED BOTH COUNSEL AND THE COURT UNNECESSARY BURDEN AND EXPENSE. THE COURT RESERVES THE RIGHT TO AWARD SANCTIONS AGAINST RAINBOW AND/OR ITS COUNSEL FOR SUCH CONDUCT; ANY MOTION FOR SUCH RELIEF MAY BE HEARD CONCURRENTLY WITH EITHER OF THE PENDING MOTIONS. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

ORDER OVERRULING OMAR GREEN'S DEMURRER TO PATRICK MCNERNEY'S CROSS-COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT RELIANT PROJECT MANAGEMENT, LLC'S DEMURRER TO PATRICK MCNERNEY'S CROSS-COMPLAINT AND TO THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT OF CROSS-COMPLAINANTS FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, MARTIN MCNERNEY DEVELOPMENT, INC., 418 JESSIE HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC AND 418 JESSIE PROPERTIES, LLC IS OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND, THIRD, AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR EQUITABLE INDEMNITY, CONTRIBUTION, AND NEGLIGENCE, AND IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR CONTRACTUAL INDEMNITY AND BREACH OF CONTRACT. THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE, EQUITABLE INDEMNITY, AND CONTRIBUTION BY ALLEGING THAT RELIANT OWED A DUTY OF CARE TO CROSS-COMPLAINANTS IN PERFORMING THE REPAIR AND MAINTENANCE WORK, BREACHED THAT DUTY BY DOING DEFECTIVE WORK (CROSS-COMPLAINT 49, 51, 59, 61, 65, 68) AND SHARES IN FAULT FOR DAMAGES SUSTAINED BY PLAINTIFF MINT COLLECTION. (HAHN V. MIRDA (2007) 147 CAL.APP.4TH 740, 747 ["NEGLIGENCE MAY BE ALLEGED IN GENERAL TERMS; THAT IS, IT IS SUFFICIENT TO ALLEGE AN ACT WAS NEGLIGENTLY DONE WITHOUT STATING THE PARTICULAR OMISSION WHICH RENDERED IT NEGLIGENT."].) THE DEMURRER TO THE CONTRACT-BASED CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 430.10(G) BECAUSE IT CANNOT BE ASCERTAINED FROM THE CROSS-COMPLAINT WHETHER THE CONTRACT(S) WAS WRITTEN OR ORAL (CROSS-COMPLAINT 63), AND THE PLEADING IS ALSO UNCERTAIN AS TO WHETHER THE BREACH OF CONTRACT AND CONTRACTUAL INDEMNITY CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BASED ON ONE OR TWO AGREEMENTS. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO CURE THESE DEFICIENCIES. COUNSEL FOR RELIANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Alliance Roofing - Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Aug-20-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Scott LaSalle, Esq. of The Aguilera Law Group, T:714.384.6600, for intervenor & opposing party, by Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: Counsel for intervenor did not contest the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT ALLIANCE ROOFING COMPANY, INC.'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement, which was adopted by the Court, as follows:

Alliance Roofing Company, Inc.'s motion for motion for determination of good faith settlement per Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 877.6 is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. The settlement satisfies the factors identified in Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. Intervenor The Travelers Indemnity Company of Connecticut's "statement of position" incorrectly asserts that the good faith determination cannot affect any of its potential claims against Alliance. "A determination by the court that the settlement was made in good faith shall bar any other joint tortfeasor or co-obligor from any further claims against the settling tortfeasor or co-obligor for equitable comparative contribution or partial or comparative indemnity based upon comparative negligence or comparative fault." (Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 877.6(c).) "Because an insurer stands in the shoes of its insured in a subrogation action, the insurer cannot pursue those types of indemnity claims against the settling tortfeasor." (Interstate Fire & Casualty Ins. Co. v. Cleveland Wrecking Co. (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 23, 32.) "However, a good faith settlement does not bar a nonsettling tortfeasor from asserting an indemnification claim against the settling defendants based on an express contract." (Id.)

The Court signed the order included with the moving papers.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion For Good Faith Settlement Order By Defendant And Cross-Defendant Sitco
Off Calendar Aug-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John N. Carr, Esq. of KIDD CARR LLP, T: 510.268.8600, for defendant & moving party;
Sergio W. Stevens, Esq. of Lee Hernandez Landrum Carlson, T: 925.939.2000, for defendants & opposiong party;
Dana M. Andreoli, Esq. of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP, for defendants and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on CROSS-DEFENDANT ACCESS SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS, INC.'S Motion For Good Faith Settlement Order.

Defendant and cross-defendant Access Systems & Solutions, Inc. Scaffold Inspection and Testing Co. USA Scaffold's ("SITCO") motion for determination of good faith settlement per Code of Civil Procedure Sec. 877.6 is granted.

SITCO has submitted evidence that it did not complete its scope of work to design and install a window washing system on the 6 Mint Building as a result of the general contractor's failure to pay; that it would have cost an estimated $25,000 for SITCO to complete that work; that SITCO's limited scope of work bore little relationship to the bulk of plaintiff's estimated cost of removing and replacing the windows at that building; and that other subcontractors with more direct responsibility have already settled with plaintiff for a substantial portion of the total cost of repairing the windows. (McGarry Decl. Paras. 12-14; Carr Decl. Para. 8, Exhs. A, A-1, B, C). SITCO has also submitted evidence that the settlement was reached as a result of arm's length bargaining, with the assistance of a mediator and a special master. (Carr Para. Decl. 9). No evidence of collusion or fraud has been submitted.

Under these circumstances, the non-settling defendants have failed to meet their burden to show that the settlement amount is "so far 'out of the ballpark' in relation to [Tech -Bilt] factors as to be inconsistent with the equitable objectives of the statute." (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499-500; see Cahill v. San Diego Gas & Electric Co. (2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 939, 967 [finding a settlement amount of 1% of the claimed damages was within "the ballpark of reasonable settlement amounts."].)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312..

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RAINBOW WATERPROOFING & RESTORATION CO. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication

Cross-defendant Rainbow Waterproofing & Restoration Co.'s motion for summary judgment and its alternative motion for summary adjudication of the cross-complaint is granted as to all causes of action stated by cross-complainants Fifth Historic Properties, LLC, 418 Jessie Historic Properties, LLC and 418 Jessie Properties, LLC. As to the causes of action stated by the Martin Building Company, the motion is granted as to the causes of action for equitable indemnity, contribution, and negligence and is denied as to the causes of action for express indemnity and breach of contract; it is moot as to the (dismissed) cause of action for breach of warranty.

Rainbow is entitled to summary adjudication of the causes of action for equitable indemnity/ implied contractual indemnity, contribution, and negligence stated by all cross-complainants because these claims are extinguished by the Court's June 5, 2019 order granting a determination of a good faith settlement between Rainbow and Plaintiff Mint Collection-410-418 Jessie Street Condominium Owners' Association. (Code Civ. Proc. 877.6(c).) The negligence cause of action is extinguished because the negligence cause of action is a disguised claim for equitable indemnity. (See Gackstetter v. Frawley (2006) 135 Cal.App.4th 1257, 1274 ["a party may not avoid a section 877.6 motion by providing different labels for what are in reality indemnity or contribution claims."]; The Ratcliff Architects v. Vanir Construction Management, Inc. (2001) 88 Cal.App.4th 595, 607 [agreeing that project architect's negligence claim against construction managers "is essentially a claim for indemnification and an attempt to avoid the consequences of the good faith settlement. To permit [architect's] claim would subvert California's public policy of encouraging good faith settlement.").

Summary adjudication is granted on the express indemnity and breach of contract causes of action stated by the McNerney LLC's because the undisputed facts show that there was no contract between the parties.

Triable issues of fact preclude summary adjudication on MBC's causes of action for express contractual indemnity and breach of contract. Although Rainbow did not execute MBC's proposed Subcontract, there are triable issues of material fact regarding the terms of the contract between MBC and Rainbow. A trier of fact could find that Rainbow accepted the Subcontract through its conduct based on evidence that: no price was specified in the Letter of Intent; payment was made according to the terms and schedule in the Subcontract; Rainbow added MBC as an additional insured as required by the Subcontract; the change orders referred to the existing "Original Construction Agreement" and "Original Contract Amount"; and Rainbow's rejection of the subcontract was not communicated to MBC (McNerney Decl. 5, 7-9; Andreolli Decl. 5; Abell Decl. Exh. D). (Civ. Code 1584, 1589).

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-01-2019 Continued To Jun-25-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RAINBOW WATERPROOFING & RESTORATION CO.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication.

Continued on the Court's own motion to June 25, 2019.

Defendant and Cross-Defendant Rainbow Waterproofing & Restoration Co. filed its motion for summary judgment or in the alternative for summary adjudication on February 15, 2019. On April 25, it filed a motion for a good faith determination as to its settlement with Plaintiff, which is currently set for hearing on June 5, 2019. According to counsel to the Developer Defendants and Patrick McNerney, Rainbow refused their requests to withdraw its summary judgment motion or to continue the hearing date on that motion pending the Court's decision on the good faith settlement motion so as to allow them to avoid preparing and filing an opposition to the motion that might be unnecessary or mooted in part. On April 29, however, Rainbow then filed an ex parte application seeking to continue the hearing on the summary judgment motion. However, its counsel failed to appear on the Court's ex parte calendar on April 30, the day before the scheduled hearing on the motion.

The hearing is continued on the Court's own motion because the disposition of the summary judgment motion necessarily will depend in part on the Court's ruling on the good faith settlement motion. However, the Court admonishes Rainbow that its conduct has caused both counsel and the Court unnecessary burden and expense. The Court reserves the right to award sanctions against Rainbow and/or its counsel for such conduct; any motion for such relief may be heard concurrently with either of the pending motions.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Scott Tate, Esq. 415-437-1800 of the Miller Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Dana Andreoli, Esq. 415-421-3400 of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP for Cross Complainants Martin McNerney Development, Inc., Fifth Historic Property LLC, 418 Jessie Historic Properties LLC, 418 Jessie Properties LLC, Patrick McNerney
Sergio Stevens, Esq. 925-939-2000 of Lee Hernandez Landrum Carlson for Cross Defendants MBC, 418 Jessie Historic,418 Jessie Properties LLC, Fifth Historic.
John Meehan, Esq. 415-544-1900 of Shook Hardy & Bacon LLP for Defendants Simplex Grinnell through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues. Defendant argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT RELIANT PROJECT MANAGEMENT, LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT.

Cross-defendant Reliant Project Management, LLC's demurrer to Patrick McNerney's cross-complaint and to the first amended cross-complaint of Cross-Complainants Fifth Historic Properties, LLC, Martin McNerney Development, Inc., 418 Jessie Historic Properties, LLC and 418 Jessie Properties, LLC is overruled as to the second, third, and fifth causes of action for equitable indemnity, contribution, and negligence, and is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the first and fourth causes of action for contractual indemnity and breach of contract. The first amended cross-complaint sufficiently pleads causes of action for negligence, equitable indemnity, and contribution by alleging that Reliant owed a duty of care to cross-complainants in performing the repair and maintenance work, breached that duty by doing defective work (Cross-Complaint 49, 51, 59, 61, 65, 68) and shares in fault for damages sustained by plaintiff Mint Collection. (Hahn v. Mirda (2007) 147 Cal.App.4th 740, 747 ["negligence may be alleged in general terms; that is, it is sufficient to allege an act was negligently done without stating the particular omission which rendered it negligent."].) The demurrer to the contract-based causes of action is sustained pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 430.10(g) because it cannot be ascertained from the cross-complaint whether the contract(s) was written or oral (Cross-Complaint 63), and the pleading is also uncertain as to whether the breach of contract and contractual indemnity causes of action are based on one or two agreements. Cross-complainants are given leave to amend to cure these deficiencies.

Counsel for Reliant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Their First Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
T. Scott Tate, Esq., The Miller Law Firm, Telephone 415-437-1800, appearing for Plaintiff;
Sergio w. Stevens, Esq., Lee Hernandez Landrum, et al Telephone 925-939-2000, appearing for Defendant/Cross Defendant MBC;
Dana Andreoli, Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas, et al, Telephone 415-421-3400, appearing for Defendant/Cross Defendant Martin McNernery Development Inc., et al;
Cynthia Shambaugh, esq., Law Offices of Thoms J. Burns, Telephone 415-2772300, appearing for Defendnat/Cross Defendnat Broadway Mechanical Contractors;(CourtCall)

Judge Schulman discloses that he is personally acquainted with Jeffrey Lowenthal, one of Defendants' counsel. This will not interfere with his ability to fairly and impartially decide this matter.

Ruling - ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC,, MARTIN MCNERNEY DEVELOPMENT, INC.,, 418 JESSIE HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC,, 418 JESSIE PROPERTIES, LLC's Motion For Leave To File Their First Amended Cross-Complaint.

Defendants Fifth Historic Properties, LLC,, Martin Mcnerney Development, Inc., 418 Jessie Historic Properties, LLC, 418 Jessie Properties, LLC's motion for leave to file a first amended cross-complaint is granted. California's liberal policy in favor of allowing amendments to pleadings, the interest in judicial economy, and plaintiff's failure to show any resulting prejudice from allowing the amendment all favor granting the motion. The viability of defendants' cross-claims against plaintiff, including the applicability and the scope of the bar under Civ. Code 5985, may be properly tested in motions attacking the sufficiency of the pleadings. Moreover, plaintiff represents that it intends to demur to co-defendant Patrick McNerney's cross-complaint, which raises identical issues. (See Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 760 ["we believe that the better course of action would have been to allow [plaintiff] to amend the complaint and then let the parties test its legal sufficiency in other appropriate proceedings."]; see also Weil and Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2018) 6:644 ["Ordinarily, the judge will not consider the validity of the proposed amended pleading in deciding whether to grant leave to amend. Grounds for demurrer or motion to strike are premature. After leave to amend is granted, the opposing party will have the opportunity to attack the validity of the amended pleading."].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion For Leave To Add Cause Of Action For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And To Substitute Patrick Mcnerney As Doe 1
Off Calendar Dec-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
T. Scott Tate, Esq. 415-437-1800 of the Miller Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Sergio Stevens, Esq. 925-939-2000 of Lee Hernandez Landrum Carlson for Defendant MBC.
Dana Andreoli, Esq. 415-421-3400 of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvares & Smith for Defendant Fifth Historic Properties.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares it's views. Both sides argue. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM's Motion For Leave To Add Cause Of Action For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And To Substitute Patrick Mcnerney As Doe 1.

The tenatative ruling is adopted with a clarification. Plaintiff's motion for leave to add cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty and to substitute Patrick McNerney as Doe 1 is granted. Granting the motion comports with California's policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings absent a clear showing of prejudice to another party. Neither Mr. McNerney nor any other party has shown that he will be prejudiced by the granting of this motion. Because there is sufficient time to take discovery and for motion practice before trial, any claim of prejudice is unpersuasive. The arguments that the motion is a misuse of the Doe procedure and the proposed new claim lacks merit as a matter of law are rejected on this motion, but may be re-raised by appropriate motion or at trial.

To clarify, the Court is allowing thhe addition of an eleventh cause of action against Mr. McNerney on the allegation that he personally breached fiduciary duties that he personally owed to the HOA and that the HOA was ignorant of that claim as of the filing of its complaint; The Court is not allowing any allegations that Mr. McNerney is primarily liable, as distinct from liable as an alter ego, of any of the claims that remain in the case that are labeled causes of action one through ten. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Cross Defendant Sun Roofing & Construction Co., Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Nov-13-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
T. Scott Tate, Esq. 415-437-1800 of the Miller Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Jeffrey lowenthal, Esq. 415-421-3400 of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP for Defendant Martin McNerney Development.
Sergio Stevens, Esq. 925-939-2000 of Lee Hernandez Landrum Carlson for Defendant MBC
Erik Faussner, Esq. 415-981-5411 of Bledsoe for Defendant Sun Roofing.
Cynthia Shambaugh, Esq. 415-227-2346 for Defendant Broadway Mechanical Contractors.
Leeh Dibello, Esq. 415-981-6630 of Low Ball Lynch for Defendant B.A. Retro through Court Call.
Robert Davis, Esq. 925-400-9575 of Collinsworth Specht Calkins & Giampaoli for Defendant Cupertino Electric through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Cross Defendant Sun Roofing & Construction Co., Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.Cross-defendant's Sun Roofing & Construction Co., Inc.'s motion for summary judgment on the MBC Parties' cross-complaint is granted. Per the October 2, 2018 order, the only remaining causes of action in the MBC Parties' cross-complaint alleged against Sun Roofing are the first, fourth and fifth causes of action for express contractual indemnity, breach of contract, and breach of warranty. As to the cause of action for express contractual indemnity, the MBC Parties have not produced evidence that the contract faxed to Sun Roofing, signed by Sun Roofing and returned for signature to MBC and signed by MBC contained the pages with the General Subcontractor Provisions. The evidence that MBC has a business practice of mailing the contract documents as well, while sending a fax, does not create a triable dispute whether at the time Sun Roofing signed the contract, Sun Roofing was clearly directed where to find the provisions or that they were known or easily available to it, therefore there is no valid incorporation by reference of the indemnity provisions into the MBC -Sun Roofing subcontract. (Perez v. DirecTV Group Holdings, LLC (C.D. Cal. 2017) 251 F.Supp.3d 1328, 1339). MBC cannot show damages for its breach of contract and breach of warranty claims because the good faith settlement determination precludes plaintiff from seeking liability from MBC for any damages caused by Sun Roofing's work on the project and MBC through these causes of action seeks to impose liability on Sun Roofing based on Sun Roofing's share of fault to plaintiff. There is no evidence of a contractual relation between the developer entities and Sun Roofing and MBC has no express contractual indemnity liability to the developer entities.

Counsel for Sun Roofing is required to prepare a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g), CRC 3.1312 and repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
T. Scott Tate, Esq. 415-437-1800 of the Miller Law Firm for Plaintiff Mint Collection.
Jack D. Lynch, Esq. 408-977-1911 of Bustamante & Gagliasso APC for Cross Defendant Station House C1 through Court call.
Sergio Stevens, Esq. 925-939-2000 of Lee Hernandez Landrum & Garofalo for Defendant Fifth Historic Properties through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT STATION HOUSE C1, LLC,, VC FUND C-1, LLC, VC MULTIFAMILY PORTFOLIO VENTURE GP, LLC's SPECIAL DEMURRERS TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling in all respects except for the 1st cause of action.
The Demurrer is sustained with 30 days leave to amend as to the 1st cause of action.

Defendants Station House C1, LLC, VC Multifamily Portfolio Venture GP, LLC and VC Fund C-1, LLC's demurrer to the first through fourth and sixth through tenth causes of action in the second amended complaint is: a) sustained without leave to amend as to the the third cause of action for strict liability, the sixth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, the seventh cause of action for violation of governing documents, the eighth cause of action for unlawful LLC distributions, the ninth cause of action for breach of contract, and the tenth cause of action for negligence; and b) sustained with thirty days leave to amend as to the first cause of action for violation of residential building standards, second cause of action for negligence and the fourth cause of action for breach of implied warranty of merchantability. The court has previously sustained demurrers to the first, third, and eighth causes of action without leave to amend as to defendants who are identically situated to the demurring defendants as to these claims and thus the demurrer to those claims is sustained without leave to amend. In their opposition memorandum plaintiff agreed to dismiss the sixth, seventh, ninth and tenth causes of action as to the demurring defendants and thus the demurrer to those claims is sustained without leave to amend. The second and fourth causes of action do not allege sufficient ultimate facts to state viable claims against the demurring defendants. If they can do so in good faith, plaintiff is given leave to allege ultimate facts supporting the second and fourth causes of action against the demurring defendants. As stated in a previous demurrer order, plaintiff should allege why it contends each of these defendants is liable instead of lumping them all together in a collectively defined term.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Also, the Court orders Plaintiff to submit a proposed order withdrawing/striking the third amended complaint filed.

Matter called - PLAINTIFF MINT COLLECTION-410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM OWNERS' ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO SUBSTITUTE PATRICK MCNERNEY AS DOES 1 AND 101 AND VERITAS INVESTMENTS, INC, AS DOES 2 AND 102.

The matter is not contested, no argument is held. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: Plaintiff Mint Collection-410-418 Jessie Street Condominium Owners' Association's motion for leave to substitute Patrick McNerney as Does 1 and 101 and Veritas Investments, Inc, as Does 2 and 102 is denied without prejudice to being refiled as an ex parte application if within the next two months the trial date is continued to a day on or after May 6, 2019. Any legal prejudice to Mr. McNerney and Veritas by the granting of the motion is eliminated if the trial is continued to May or June 2019 as Mint Collection expects will happen, but has not yet occurred nor is there a pending motion to continue the trial. Absent a trial continuance, the prejudice to Mr. McNerney and Veritas from having to prepare for a trial two months away and being deprived of the opportunity to file a summary judgment motion requires denial of the motion. If the trial is continued, granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings at any stage in a case. Mint Collection's alter ego allegations, even if not legally sufficient, do not show that Mint Collection will be unable to viably allege alter ego liability against Mr. McNerney and Veritas. Counsel for Mint Collection is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Travelers Motion To Strike Altamont Electrics Cross Complaint, Memo Of P&A, Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Aug-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

INTERVENOR THE TRAVELERS INDEMNITY COMPANY OF CONNECTICUT's Special Motion To Strike Altamont Electrics Cross Complaint Pursuant to CCP 425.16 (anti-Slapp)

Defendant The Travelers Indemnity Company of Connecticut's anti-SLAPP motion to strike the entirety of the cross-complaint filed by Altamont Electric Co. is granted. Travelers satisfied its first prong burden of showing that Altamont's cross-complaint arises from petitioning activity -- specifically, activity pertaining to the settlement of litigation -- protected by the anti-SLAPP statute. Altamont has not satisfied its burden to show an exception to protected activity such as the commercial speech exemption or the public interest exemption. Nor has Altamont satisfied its second prong burden of showing that there is minimal merit to its cross-complaint. Altamont's failure to provide any evidence to show that it can prove its cross-complaint and its sole reliance on the allegations of the cross-complaint precludes it from meeting its second prong burden. Altamont's argument that its allegations show that Travelers committed extortion or other illegal activity and thus its cross-complaint is outside of the anti-SLAPP statute is unavailing. Assertedly "illegal" speech or petitioning is outside the protections of the anti-SLAPP statute only when a defendant either concedes or the evidence conclusively establishes that the defendant's conduct was illegal as a matter of law, and there is neither a concession nor conclusive (nor any) evidence of illegality in this case. (Flatley v. Mauro (2006) 39 Cal. 4th 312, 320).

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Susan Gill, Esq. 619-881-0108 of Gill & Rhoades for Intervenor AXIS Surplus Insurance through Court Call.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC,, MARTIN MCNERNEY DEVELOPMENT, INC.,, 418 JESSIE HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC,, 418 JESSIE PROPERTIES, LLC'S Limited Motion To Contest Broadway Mechanical-Contractors, Inc.'S Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement And Order Dismissing And Barring Cross-Complaints.

Defendants and cross-complainants Martin McNerney Development, Inc., Fifth Historic Properties, LLC, 418 Jessie Historic Properties, LLC and 418 Jessie Properties, LLC's motion to contest good faith settlement is denied. The movants acknowledge that the settlement is in good faith and bars further claims for equitable contribution or equitable indemnity. While a good faith determination bars claims of implied contractual indemnity, it does not bar the movants from continuing to prosecute their first, fourth and fifth causes of action in their cross-complaint. Those claims can be construed as claims for express contractual indemnity. The court cannot resolve on this motion the contractual interpretation issues that the parties' pleadings raise. A motion for summary judgment/adjudication or a trial is necessary to resolve the scope of the parties' respective express indemnity rights and obligations.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Not Reported. =(302/HK)

MINI MINUTES:

Atty. T. Scott Tate of the Miller Law Firm, Tel. No. 1-415-437-1800 appearing
for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Jeffrey H. Lowenthal & Atty. Dana Andreoli of Steyer Lowenthal & Boodrookas LLP Tel. No.1-415-421-3400;
Atty. Sergio Stevens of The Lee Hernandez Law Firm appearing for te Defendants MBC,
Fifth Historic et. al.

RULING - A R G U E D; Defendants Martin McNerney Development, Inc., Fifth Historic Properties, LLC, 418 Jessie Historic Properties, LLC and 418 Jessie Properties, LLC's demurrer to the first, third, sixth and eighth causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Mint Collection-410-418 Jessie Street Condominium Owners' Association is: a); sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the first cause of action for violation of residential building standards to identify with exquisite specificity any portion of either of these 2 buildings which are alleged to be both original or new contruction; sustained without leave to amend as to third and sixth causes of action for strict liability and breach of fiduciary duty; and b) sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the eighth cause of action for unlawful LLC distributions. The third cause of action fails because strict liability only applies to mass-produced homes and the construction involved in this case did not involve mass-produced homes. The sixth cause of action fails because there are no facts alleged in the FAC, nor is reasonably possible that there could be any alleged facts, supporting a fiduciary duty owed to the Association by any of the demurring defendants. The eighth cause fails because there are no facts alleged showing any unlawful distribution, but leave to amend is granted because it is reasonably possible that the Association may be able to allege such facts. The court also allowed the filing of a new motion to be heard at the same time with the current motion in this case on June 7, 2018. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 31312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk

Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-20-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Thomas V. Perea of the law offices of The Aguilera Law Firm (714.384.6600) appears as counsel for interventor/moving party, The Travelers Indemnity Company of Connecticut

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

INTERVENOR THE TRAVELERS INDEMNITY COMPANY OF CONNECTICUT Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint is DENIED. As Travelers concedes, it has been settled law in the California appellate courts for at least 20 years that "[e]quitable contribution only applies between insurers." (Aerojet-General Corp. v. Transport Indem. Co. (1997) 17 Cal.4th 38, 72; Herrick Corp. v. Canadian Ins. Co. (1994) 29 Cal.App.4th 753, 763-66.) Travelers relies on two "tentative rulings" by trial courts in other counties. However, the first is dicta in a passage that Travelers concedes is factually incorrect. The second tentative relies on a footnote that never uses the words "insurance," "insurers" or "equitable contribution," but supposedly overrules Aerojet and Herrick despite not mentioning those decisions.

The motion is denied as to McNerney as a new defendant. Two new claims may be added to the complaint as to other defendants. Counsel for defendant is to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.
